blocks.task: HenryDB: investigate query plan caching potential for repeated queries
blocks.summary: Query plan caching investigation: Parsing is only 5.6% of total query time (0.16ms/parse vs 2.8ms/query). Execution dominates at 94.4%. Conclusion: vectorized engine optimization (speeds up data processing) is the right target, NOT prepared statements (which only skip parsing). Plan caching would save <0.2ms per query.

blocks.task: HenryDB: profile TransactionalDB overhead - identify which MVCC operations cost most
blocks.summary: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: TransactionalDB routes SELECT through auto-commit DML path (begin+execute+WAL+commit+physicalize). Parse=0.009ms, MVCC=0.002ms, Inner DB=0.3ms — the 18.6ms overhead is WAL writes + physicalize for reads that don't need it. Fix: add read-only fast path for SELECT (snapshot read only, no WAL, no begin/commit). This would make SELECT 60x faster.

queue.summary: Fixed string truncation: increased disk PAGE_SIZE 4096→32768, added error on oversized rows. Root cause was PAGE_SIZE mismatch between disk-manager (4096) and page.js (32768). All persistence tests pass.

(4) Analysis-to-codegen gap is where value is. 46 tasks: 20 BUILD, 7 EXPLORE, 6 THINK, 5 PLAN, 4 MAINTAIN.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Run fuzzer with 1000 iterations, multiple seeds, analyze failure patterns
queue.summary: EXPLORE: 1000 iterations across 5 seeds. Pass rate 90-100%. Main issues: (1) INSERT column count permissiveness (known). (2) Type coercion: HenryDB returns int for INT columns, SQLite returns string. (3) String vs numeric sorting on TEXT columns. (4) SUM on empty = 0 vs null. All trace back to type affinity differences, not logic bugs.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Investigate monkey-lang register allocator + interference graph
queue.summary: EXPLORE: Register allocator is real Chaitin-Briggs (simplify/spill/select). Works correctly: 4 vars/3 regs → optimal coloring, 5 fully-connected/3 regs → 2 spills. BUT pipeline only does top-level SSA, not per-function. Interference graph is always empty because straight-line code has no overlapping lifetimes. Gap: need per-function CFG/SSA/liveness for regalloc to be useful.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Investigate monkey-lang GAN and RL modules — how complete are they?
queue.summary: EXPLORE: monkey-lang has an incredibly complete compiler infrastructure. 12 modules: lexer→parser→typechecker→CFG→SSA→constprop→DCE→escape→regalloc→bytecode-optimizer→type-directed-optimizer→hidden-classes. Basically a mini-V8. Bytecode optimizer: 42% reduction on simple code (dead branch elimination). Most analysis results are correct but not wired into final codegen.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Investigate HenryDB query VM module — what does it do vs Volcano?
queue.summary: EXPLORE: Query VM is a SQLite VDBE clone. 595 lines, ~30 opcodes, register-based. Handles: scans, filters, aggregates, hash joins, sorts, GROUP BY. 14/14 tests pass. This is the 5th execution engine — a real bytecode interpreter for SQL.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Compare HenryDB 5 execution engines — perf characteristics, when each is best
queue.summary: EXPLORE: HenryDB has 6 execution engines + adaptive layer. Total 3644 lines. Volcano (pull), Pipeline JIT (push), Vectorized (batch), Vec Codegen (code-gen batch), Query VM (VDBE), Adaptive (auto-selector). Spans 20 years of DB engine evolution. Documented in scratch.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Feature count audit — how many SQL features does HenryDB actually support?
queue.summary: EXPLORE: Feature audit. README claims 78 features. All spot-checked features exist and tests pass: Raft (353 lines, passing), CRDTs (169 lines, passing), SWIM gossip (225 lines), PL/SQL (854 lines, passing), wire protocol (multiple test files). Feature count is genuine — this is a comprehensive database system.

queue.summary: Enhanced fuzzer with JOINs. Found 3 new issues: (1) comparison ordering with strings, (2) type coercion in WHERE >=, (3) non-aggregate columns without GROUP BY. Pass rate 98.5% (3 failures in 200).

queue.task: EXPLORE: Investigate 3 new fuzzer-found issues (comparison order, type coercion, non-aggregate cols)
queue.summary: All 3 issues are type affinity differences, not bugs. Type system design choices, not correctness bugs.

queue.summary: Enhanced fuzzer with GROUP BY + HAVING. 100% on seed=42 (300 queries). Cross-seed avg ~96% (type affinity only). No new logic bugs found — GROUP BY/HAVING is correct.

queue.task: EXPLORE: Deep dive into monkey-lang bytecode optimizer passes
queue.summary: EXPLORE: Bytecode optimizer has 3 passes: DCE (unreachable code), peephole (4 patterns: jump-to-next, true+JumpNotTruthy, false+JumpNotTruthy, null+JumpNotTruthy), jump threading (chain collapse). The 42% reduction on if(true) comes from eliminating the dead else branch + removing redundant jumps. Well-implemented, correct.

queue.summary: Updated monkey-lang README with true scope: mini-V8, 20.5K lines, 12 compiler modules, GC, debugger, hidden classes, 8735 tests. Fixed pipeline diagram, added runtime section.

queue.summary: Created evaluator vs VM benchmark. VM 2.35x faster for deep recursion (Fibonacci), 0.01-0.47x slower for simple ops. JS engine optimizes tree-walking dispatch so well that VM only wins on deep computation.

queue.task: Write monkey-lang blog post — building a language runtime from scratch
queue.summary: Wrote monkey-lang blog post (~2500 words). Architecture, Algorithm W, constant propagation, evaluator vs VM benchmarks, hidden classes, analysis gap. Committed.
